title: EVgo Q2 2023 Earnings Results Beat Expectations
text: EVgo reported second-quarter 2023 earnings results that beat Wall Street expectations. Loss per share was 8 cents versus 27 cents expected, and revenue was $50.6 million versus $29.6 million expected. The company reported a net loss of $21.5 million, or 8 cents per share. Network throughput grew 147% year over year to 24.9 gigawatt-hours in Q2. Revenue from eXtend totaled approximately $33.3 million in Q2, or nearly 66% of EVgo's total revenue for the period. The company's eXtend private-label charging business boomed, demonstrating strong growth in commercial partnerships. EVgo updated its full-year guidance, expecting revenue between $120 million and $150 million, up from $105 million to $150 million in prior guidance. The company also guided for an adjusted EBITDA loss of between $68 million and $78 million, a narrower range than the $60 million to $78 million in earlier guidance. Shares were up about 8% in after-hours trading following the report, reflecting positive market reaction to the earnings beat.
